linearization: param(theta) = nominal + max(theta,0)*(Up - nominal) - max(-theta,0)*(nominal - Down)
comment: Canonical mapping from rabbit nuisance name to physical NP parameter for the dilepton-Z alphaS analysis (LatticeNoConstraints CS + lowercase TMD). Generated 2026-05-11. Source of truth for translating rabbit pulls to physical lambda values; used by post-processing scripts to make tables and by the np_monotonicity regularizer to enforce walls.

important_conventions.CS_side_Up_Down_convention: STANDARD: Up = larger physical value. Fixed 2026-05-11 in rabbit_theory_helper.py (line 706-715): the iteration order is now ['Down','Up'] inside the name loop, so the smaller-value template (e.g. lambda2_nu0.0538) is paired with 'Down' and the larger (lambda2_nu0.1202) with 'Up'. Positive rabbit pull -> larger physical lambda. CAVEAT: HDF5s produced BEFORE the fix used the inverted convention -- in those, scetlibNPgammaLambda2Up was paired with lambda2_nu0.0538. Identify the convention used by checking the hist_label_at_Up/Down entries against the actual histogram syst-axis values.
important_conventions.TMD_delta_lambda2_nominal_is_zero: In the LatticeNoConstraints branch, the histmaker uses delta_lambda2 nominal = 0 (NO y-dependence in nominal). Templates at +/-0.02 absolute. This is DIFFERENT from the AN-quoted Delta_Lambda_2 = 0.125 GeV^2 which applies to the uppercase 'Delta_Lambda' parametrization branch. The 0.125 baseline is NOT included in the LatticeNoConstraints templates.
important_conventions.TMD_lambda4_asymmetric: Lambda_4 templates at [0.01, 0.12] are absolute, asymmetric around AN nominal 0.06. delta_up != delta_down.
important_conventions.CS_helper_internal_scale_10: rabbit_theory_helper.py:738-739 applies kfactor=10 to all scetlibNPgamma* nuisances under LatticeNoConstraints. Equivalent to a 10x wider effective prior on rabbit-theta. Whether this amplifies the physical interpretation of a unit-theta pull depends on convention; this map encodes the physical values at theta=+/-1 IGNORING the scale=10 (i.e., as if scale=1).

scetlibNPgammaLambda2.convention: Standard: Up = larger physical value (post-2026-05-11 fix)

scetlibNPgammaLambda4.convention: Standard: Up = larger physical value (post-2026-05-11 fix)

scetlibNPgammaLambdaInf.convention: Standard: Up = larger physical value (post-2026-05-11 fix)

chargeVgenNP0scetlibNPZdelta_lambda2.convention: Standard: Up = larger physical value. NOMINAL = 0 (NOT the AN's 0.125 which applies to a different parametrization branch).

chargeVgenNP0scetlibNPZlambda4.convention: Standard: Up = larger physical value; asymmetric range

lambda_6.role: CS extension: coeff of b_T^6 / lambda_inf

Lambda_6.role: TMD extension: coeff of b_T^5 / Lambda_inf
